channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Puzzle Games and News Headlines: A Blend of Entertainment and Current Affairs
item.description: The provided text is a mix of puzzle game titles, news headlines, and various topics. It includes references to popular puzzles like 'Loop The Loop Puzzle Circuit' and 'Daily Crossword,' alongside cur...

item.title: Philippines Faces Sharp Fuel Price Hike Amid Global Tensions
item.description: The Philippines is set to experience a significant surge in fuel prices as the government announces a planned increase of over P10 per liter for both diesel and kerosene. This decision comes amid heig...

item.title: PDP and APC Submit Governorship Candidates to INEC Ahead of Deadline
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) and the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC) have started uploading their governorship and state Houses of Assembly candidates to the Independent National Electora...

item.title: Retired Bishop Warns of Political Crisis and Calls for Release of Opposition Leader
item.description: Bishop Dr Glorius Shoo, a retired religious leader from Tanzania, has warned that the nation is facing a profound crisis of hope driven by political deception and exclusion. In an exclusive interview ...

item.title: Andy Burnham Assumes Role as UK Prime Minister Amid Economic and Political Challenges
item.description: Andy Burnham is set to become the sixth British prime minister since 2016, taking over from Keir Starmer after the latter's resignation due to leadership scandals. Burnham, 56, faces immediate challen...

item.title: Philippines denounces AI-generated video falsely portraying Filipinos as monkeys in South China Sea dispute
item.description: The Philippine Palace has strongly condemned an AI-generated video released by Chinese state media that depicts Filipinos as monkeys, calling it racist and offensive. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. pu...

item.title: Oscar Sudi Defends IEBC Neutrality After Ol Kalou By-Election Victory
item.description: UDA MP Oscar Sudi has used the landslide victory of DCP's Sammy Douglas Kamau Waweru in the Ol Kalou by-election to argue that the Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) remains neutra...

item.title: Sony Ends Physical Game Production, Impacting Local Retailers
item.description: Sony has announced plans to cease production of physical video game copies by 2028, marking a significant shift in the gaming industry. This decision affects local retailers like Hi-Tech Gametraders i...

item.title: States Aren’t Richer Despite Increased Allocations Due to Inflation
item.description: The article argues that Nigerian states aren’t actually richer despite increased monthly allocations from the federal government. It highlights how inflation has eroded purchasing power, making it har...

item.title: Dimple Yadav Joins Protest Against NEET Paper Leaks and Government Allegations
item.description: Dimple Yadav, along with Samajwadi Party MPs, marched from Parliament to Jantar Mantar to join the CJP's protest against NEET paper leaks and alleged educational system irregularities. She criticized ...

item.title: Burnham's Leadership Style and Institutional Challenges in UK Politics
item.description: The article examines Andy Burnham's assertion that he will implement policies more swiftly than his predecessor Keir Starmer, highlighting systemic delays within the British government. It contrasts S...

item.title: Kerala court awards death sentence to convict in Nenmara double murder case
item.description: A court in Kerala has sentenced 61-year-old Chenthamara to death after convicting him in the 2025 Nenmara double murder case involving the killing of his neighbour Sudhakaran and Sudhakaran's mother, ...

item.title: Diana Rigg's Evolution as an Actress in the 1970s
item.description: Diana Rigg (July 20, 1938 – September 10, 2020) was a British actress renowned for her elegance, wit, and versatility. During the 1970s, she transitioned from her 1960s pop-culture fame as Emma Peel i...

item.title: Boat Accident in Jigawa Leaves One Dead, Many Missing
item.description: A tragic boat accident occurred in Malamawa community, Yan Dutse district of Ringim Local Government Area, Jigawa State, leaving one person dead and many missing. The incident happened on Sunday when ...

item.title: Kapamilya Stars Head to Sydney for ASAP XP Australia 2026
item.description: The 'ASAP XP Australia 2026' event is set to bring prominent Kapamilya stars to Sydney, Australia, as part of its global outreach efforts. This event, which marks the 2026 edition of the popular Kapam...

item.title: Understanding why children resist wearing jumpers in the cold
item.description: Children often claim they aren't cold when refusing to wear jumpers, but this isn't about stubbornness. Research explains that kids experience cold differently from adults due to factors like higher m...

item.title: Musician Jennifer Finch Dies at 59 After Losing Battle with Brain Cancer
item.description: Kenyan music icon Jennifer Finch, best known as the bassist and vocalist of influential punk band L7, has passed away at age 59 after a fierce battle with an aggressive form of brain cancer. Her death...

item.title: Matlala and Co-Defendants Face Trial Over Alleged Assassination Attempts in South Africa
item.description: The trial of Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala, an alleged member of the 'Big Five' cartel, and four co-accused individuals has commenced in the Johannesburg High Court. Matlala and two alleged hitmen remain in ...

item.title: Autism Alliance of Canada advances coordinated advocacy for societal inclusion
item.description: The Autism Alliance of Canada, formed in 2007 by five organizations advocating for autism support, has grown to include 1,500 members. The alliance focuses on creating equal rights and opportunities f...

item.title: Spain wins World Cup final against Argentina after violent clashes and emotional moments
item.description: Spain secured their second World Cup title with a 1-0 victory over Argentina in a dramatic final that descended into violence after full-time. Ferran Torres' extra-time goal sealed the win for Luis de...
